tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

{"total_count":17376,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4103136491,"number":57254,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[Generator Bug] ModelFactory @@flattenProperty: ProcessTypeForBackCompatibility discards flattened property values when param order changes","body":"## Bug Summary

When a model has `@@flattenProperty` applied AND the previous SDK contract had the same flattened parameters in a **different order**, the generated ModelFactory method accepts flattened parameters but passes `default` for the Properties wrapper — **silently discarding all values**.

## Root Cause

A timing/ordering issue in the generator pipeline (`CSharpGen.cs`):

1. **`EnsureBuilt()`** — factory method has `properties` wrapper param
2. **`FlattenPropertyVisitor`** — correctly replaces `properties` with flattened sub-params AND builds the ternary `new Properties(...)` in the body ✅
3. **`ProcessTypeForBackCompatibility()`** — runs **after** all visitors, detects the old SDK had the same params in a different order, calls `TryBuildMethodArgumentsForOverload` which **replaces the method body** ❌

In step 3, `ConstructMethodBody` tries to rebuild the body by matching params by name, but `"properties"` doesn't match any flattened param name (e.g. `"provisioningState"`), so it passes `default` for the Properties wrapper.

## Affected ServiceBus Models

| Model | Factory creates wrapper? | Values discarded |
|-------|--------------------------|------------------|
| `ServiceBusNamespaceData` | ✅ Correct | None |
| `ServiceBusPrivateEndpointConnectionData` | ❌ `default` | `privateEndpointId`, `connectionState`, `provisioningState` |
| `ServiceBusNamespacePatch` | ❌ `default` | `provisioningState`, `status`, `createdOn`, `updatedOn`, `serviceBusEndpoint`, `metricId`, `encryption`, `privateEndpointConnections`, `disableLocalAuth`, `alternateName` |

## Trigger Condition

The bug is triggered when:
- `@@flattenProperty` is applied to a model's `properties` field
- The old SDK (baseline) had the **exact same** flattened params in a **different order**
- No new params were added (equal count)

## Suggested Fix

In `ModelFactoryProvider.cs` method `TryBuildMethodArgumentsForOverload` (approx line 258):

`csharp
// Current: rejects equal-count reordered params
if (currentMethodParameterCount <= previousParameterCount || ...)
    return false;

// Fix: change <= to < to allow equal-count reordering
if (currentMethodParameterCount < previousParameterCount || ...)
    return false;
`

Plus use name-based parameter comparison instead of reference equality in the HashSet.

## Workaround

For `ServiceBusNamespacePatch`: reorder the `properties` field placement in the TypeSpec replacement model (move it after the `...OmitProperties<>` spread).

For `ServiceBusPrivateEndpointConnectionData`: the replacement model approach doesn't work because `@@alternateType` on an ARM resource strips its resource identity. Must use a Customized partial class to override the factory method.

## Related

- ServiceBus TypeSpec migration PR: #57113
- Spec PR: Generator] BuildGetChildResourceMethods does not pass path parameters for nested child resources","body":"## Bug Description

`ResourceClientProvider.BuildGetChildResourceMethods()` generates collection getter and `Get`/`GetAsync` methods for child resources on a parent resource class. When child resources have extra path parameters between the parent and child resource (e.g., `nestedResourceTypeFirst`), these parameters are:

1. **Not passed to the collection constructor** in the getter method body (line 480)
2. **Not included in the Get/GetAsync method signatures** (line 504-510)
3. **Not forwarded** when calling the collection getter from Get/GetAsync methods (line 515)

## Impact

This affects any ARM resource with nested children that have intermediate path segments. For example, ProviderHub has:
- `ResourceTypeRegistration` (parent)
  - `NestedResourceTypeFirstSku` (child, needs `nestedResourceTypeFirst` path param)
  - `NestedResourceTypeSecondSku` (child, needs `nestedResourceTypeFirst` + `nestedResourceTypeSecond`)
  - `NestedResourceTypeThirdSku` (child, needs 3 path params)

The generated accessor methods either compile with errors or are suppressed via `CodeGenSuppress`.

## Root Cause

**File**: `ResourceClientProvider.cs` in `Azure.Generator.Management`

**Collection getter** (line 480):
\\\csharp
// Only passes client and Id, ignores path parameters from signature
New.Instance(childResource.ResourceCollection.Type, client, thisResource.Id())
\\\

**Get method signature** (line 504-510): Only includes `collectionSignature.Parameters` (e.g., sku, cancellationToken) but not the path parameters from the collection getter's signature.

**Get method body** (line 515): Calls `thisResource.Invoke(signature.Name)` without passing path parameter arguments.

## Fix

See PR linked to this issue.","author_association":"MEMBER","user":{"login":"haiyuazhang","id":1924967,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE5MjQ5Njc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1924967?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/haiyuazhang","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/subscriptions"},"comments":0,"created_at":"2026-03-19T17:17:55Z","updated_at":"2026-03-19T17:17:55Z","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252/events","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252/labels{/name}","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252/reactions"},"node_id":"I_kwDOACyxMM70kE5B"},{"id":4102778129,"number":57250,"state":"closed","state_reason":"not_planned","locked":true,"title":"Easy","body":"### Type of issue

gement: ApiSchemaTests.CRUD playback test failure due to recording mismatch","body":"## Description

The `ApiSchemaTests.CRUD` test in `Azure.ResourceManager.ApiManagement` is failing in playback mode with `TestRecordingMismatchException`. This indicates that the recorded session is out of date and needs to be re-recorded.

## Error

```
Azure.Core.TestFramework.TestRecordingMismatchException : If this is a new recording, make sure you have pushed the recordings to the assets repository.
   at Azure.ResourceManager.ApiManagement.Tests.ApiSchemaTests.CRUD() in sdk/apimanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.ApiManagement/tests/Scenario/ApiSchemaTests.cs:line 242
```

## Impact

This test failure blocks CI for unrelated PRs that include the `apimanagement` service directory.

## Action Required

Re-record the `ApiSchemaTests.CRUD` test session and push updated recordings to the assets repository.

## Affected File

- Tag methods should not be generated when PATCH operation has no request body","body":"## Description

The current MPG (Management Plane Generator) does not check whether a PATCH operation accepts a request body before deciding to generate tag methods (`AddTag`/`SetTags`/`RemoveTag`). If a resource has a `tags` property (e.g. inherits from `TrackedResource`) and has a PATCH operation that takes **no request body**, the generator may still produce tag methods — but those methods would be non-functional since there is no way to write updated tags back to the service via the bodyless PATCH.

## Current Behavior

In `ResourceClientProvider.BuildMethods()` ([ResourceClientProvider.cs#L409](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/ResourceClientProvider.cs#L409)), the tag generation gate is:

```csharp
if (HasTags() && _readMethod is not null && updateMethodProvider is not null)
```

`updateMethodProvider` is set to non-null whenever a `ResourceOperationKind.Create` (PUT) or `ResourceOperationKind.Update` (PATCH) method exists (line 382), **regardless of whether the PATCH operation accepts a body parameter**.

The fallback path in `BaseTagMethodProvider.BuildElseStatements()` ([BaseTagMethodProvider.cs#L228](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/TagMethodProviders/BaseTagMethodProvider.cs#L228)) then does:

```csharp
var updateParam = _updateMethodProvider.Signature.Parameters
    .Where(p => !p.Type.Equals(typeof(WaitUntil))).First();
```

This assumes a body parameter exists. For a bodyless PATCH, this would either crash or produce nonsensical code.

## Expected Behavior

The generator should **skip tag method generation** when:
- The resource has no PUT (`CreateOrUpdate`) operation, **and**
- The PATCH (`Update`) operation has **no request body** (i.e. no parameter to carry tag changes)

## Evidence from Existing SDKs

Analysis of the existing generated SDKs shows this pattern clearly. Among the 34 `TrackedResourceData` resources without tag methods in the current repo:

| Category | Count |
|---|---|
| No PATCH at all | 16 |
| PATCH exists but has no body | 16 |
| Has full CRUD (anomaly, AppService only) | 2 |

**None** of the 34 have a PATCH with a body. Resources with bodyless PATCHes include:
- `ProviderMonitorSettingResource` (ProviderHub) — `UpdateAsync(CancellationToken)`
- `AppServiceCertificateOrderResource` (CertificateRegistration)
- `CosmosDBFleetResource` (CosmosDB)
- `LabPlanResource` / `LabResource` (LabServices)
- And 11 others

In contrast, **all 568** `TrackedResourceData` resources that correctly have tag methods also have either a PUT or a PATCH **with a body parameter**.

## Suggested Fix

In `ResourceClientProvider.BuildMethods()`, add a check that the update method has a meaningful body parameter before generating tag methods. For example, in `PopulateUpdateClient()` or at the tag generation gate, verify that the PATCH method's input model contains parameters beyond `WaitUntil` and `CancellationToken`.","author_association":"MEMBER","user":{"login":"haiyuazhang","id":1924967,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE5MjQ5Njc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1924967?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/haiyuazhang","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":1178448111,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Mgmt","name":"Mgmt","color":"ffeb77","description":"This {"issues":[{"number":57254,"title":"[Generator Bug] ModelFactory @@flattenProperty: ProcessTypeForBackCompatibility discards flattened property values when param order changes","body":"## Bug Summary

When a model has `@@flattenProperty` applied AND the previous SDK contract had the same flattened parameters in a **different order**, the generated ModelFactory method accepts flattened parameters but passes `default` for the Properties wrapper — **silently discarding all values**.

## Root Cause

A timing/ordering issue in the generator pipeline (`CSharpGen.cs`):

1. **`EnsureBuilt()`** — factory method has `properties` wrapper param
2. **`FlattenPropertyVisitor`** — correctly replaces `properties` with flattened sub-params AND builds the ternary `new Properties(...)` in the body ✅
3. **`ProcessTypeForBackCompatibility()`** — runs **after** all visitors, detects the old SDK had the same params in a different order, calls `TryBuildMethodArgumentsForOverload` which **replaces the method body** ❌

In step 3, `ConstructMethodBody` tries to rebuild the body by matching params by name, but `"properties"` doesn't match any flattened param name (e.g. `"provisioningState"`), so it passes `default` for the Properties wrapper.

## Affected ServiceBus Models

| Model | Factory creates wrapper? | Values discarded |
|-------|--------------------------|------------------|
| `ServiceBusNamespaceData` | ✅ Correct | None |
| `ServiceBusPrivateEndpointConnectionData` | ❌ `default` | `privateEndpointId`, `connectionState`, `provisioningState` |
| `ServiceBusNamespacePatch` | ❌ `default` | `provisioningState`, `status`, `createdOn`, `updatedOn`, `serviceBusEndpoint`, `metricId`, `encryption`, `privateEndpointConnections`, `disableLocalAuth`, `alternateName` |

## Trigger Condition

The bug is triggered when:
- `@@flattenProperty` is applied to a model's `properties` field
- The old SDK (baseline) had the **exact same** flattened params in a **different order**
- No new params were added (equal count)

## Suggested Fix

In `ModelFactoryProvider.cs` method `TryBuildMethodArgumentsForOverload` (approx line 258):

`csharp
// Current: rejects equal-count reordered params
if (currentMethodParameterCount <= previousParameterCount || ...)
    return false;

// Fix: change <= to < to allow equal-count reordering
if (currentMethodParameterCount < previousParameterCount || ...)
    return false;
`

Plus use name-based parameter comparison instead of reference equality in the HashSet.

## Workaround

For `ServiceBusNamespacePatch`: reorder the `properties` field placement in the TypeSpec replacement model (move it after the `...OmitProperties<>` spread).

For `ServiceBusPrivateEndpointConnectionData`: the replacement model approach doesn't work because `@@alternateType` on an ARM resource strips its resource identity. Must use a Customized partial class to override the factory method.

## Related

- ServiceBus TypeSpec migration PR: #57113
- Spec PR: Azure/azure-rest-api-specs#41403","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"haiyuazhang"},"created_at":"2026-03-19T17:25:42Z","updated_at":"2026-03-19T17:25:42Z"},{"number":57252,"title":"[Mgmt Generator] BuildGetChildResourceMethods does not pass path parameters for nested child resources","body":"## Bug Description

`ResourceClientProvider.BuildGetChildResourceMethods()` generates collection getter and `Get`/`GetAsync` methods for child resources on a parent resource class. When child resources have extra path parameters between the parent and child resource (e.g., `nestedResourceTypeFirst`), these parameters are:

1. **Not passed to the collection constructor** in the getter method body (line 480)
2. **Not included in the Get/GetAsync method signatures** (line 504-510)
3. **Not forwarded** when calling the collection getter from Get/GetAsync methods (line 515)

## Impact

This affects any ARM resource with nested children that have intermediate path segments. For example, ProviderHub has:
- `ResourceTypeRegistration` (parent)
  - `NestedResourceTypeFirstSku` (child, needs `nestedResourceTypeFirst` path param)
  - `NestedResourceTypeSecondSku` (child, needs `nestedResourceTypeFirst` + `nestedResourceTypeSecond`)
  - `NestedResourceTypeThirdSku` (child, needs 3 path params)

The generated accessor methods either compile with errors or are suppressed via `CodeGenSuppress`.

## Root Cause

**File**: `ResourceClientProvider.cs` in `Azure.Generator.Management`

**Collection getter** (line 480):
\\\csharp
// Only passes client and Id, ignores path parameters from signature
New.Instance(childResource.ResourceCollection.Type, client, thisResource.Id())
\\\

**Get method signature** (line 504-510): Only includes `collectionSignature.Parameters` (e.g., sku, cancellationToken) but not the path parameters from the collection getter's signature.

**Get method body** (line 515): Calls `thisResource.Invoke(signature.Name)` without passing path parameter arguments.

## Fix

See PR linked to this issue.","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"haiyuazhang"},"created_at":"2026-03-19T17:17:55Z","updated_at":"2026-03-19T17:17:55Z"},{"number":57247,"title":"ApiManagement: ApiSchemaTests.CRUD playback test failure due to recording mismatch","body":"## Description

The `ApiSchemaTests.CRUD` test in `Azure.ResourceManager.ApiManagement` is failing in playback mode with `TestRecordingMismatchException`. This indicates that the recorded session is out of date and needs to be re-recorded.

## Error

```
Azure.Core.TestFramework.TestRecordingMismatchException : If this is a new recording, make sure you have pushed the recordings to the assets repository.
   at Azure.ResourceManager.ApiManagement.Tests.ApiSchemaTests.CRUD() in sdk/apimanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.ApiManagement/tests/Scenario/ApiSchemaTests.cs:line 242
```

## Impact

This test failure blocks CI for unrelated PRs that include the `apimanagement` service directory.

## Action Required

Re-record the `ApiSchemaTests.CRUD` test session and push updated recordings to the assets repository.

## Affected File

- `sdk/apimanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.ApiManagement/tests/Scenario/ApiSchemaTests.cs`","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"ArcturusZhang"},"labels":["API Management","Service Attention","Mgmt"],"created_at":"2026-03-19T09:29:02Z","updated_at":"2026-03-19T09:51:59Z"},{"number":57243,"title":"MPG: Tag methods should not be generated when PATCH operation has no request body","body":"## Description

- `ProviderMonitorSettingResource` (ProviderHub) — `UpdateAsync(CancellationToken)`

- `CosmosDBFleetResource` (CosmosDB)
- `LabPlanResource` / `LabResource` (LabServices)
- And 11 others

For example, in `PopulateUpdateClient()` or at the tag generation gate, verify that the PATCH method's input model contains parameters beyond `WaitUntil` and `CancellationToken`.","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"haiyuazhang"},"labels":["Mgmt","CodeGen"],"comments":1,"created_at":"2026-03-19T06:00:05Z","updated_at":"2026-03-19T16:18:09Z"},{"number":57231,"title":"[Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding] UpdateAnalyzer/UpdateAnalyzerAsync docs should clarify which properties are updatable","body":"## Description

The `UpdateAnalyzer` and `UpdateAnalyzerAsync` methods (and their protocol overloads) currently have XML doc summaries that say only "Update analyzer properties" without specifying **which** properties can actually be updated.

The underlying REST API uses `PATCH` with `application/merge-patch+json`, and **only `description` and `tags`** are accepted by the service. All other properties (`models`, `fieldSchema`, `config`, `baseAnalyzerId`, `dynamicFieldSchema`, `processingLocation`, `knowledgeSources`) are create-only — they are silently ignored by the server even though it returns HTTP 200.

This has caused customer confusion — see #57215.

## Requested Improvements

1. **API doc (`` / ``)**: Add a `` section to `UpdateAnalyzer`/`UpdateAnalyzerAsync` listing the updatable properties and noting that other properties require `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true`.

2. **Sample doc (Sample08_UpdateAnalyzer.md)**: Add a note in the "About updating analyzers" section explaining that only `description` and `tags` are updatable via this method, and reference `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true` for changing `models`, `fieldSchema`, etc.

## Updatable Properties

| Property | Updatable via `UpdateAnalyzer` (PATCH)? |
|---|---|
| `description` | **Yes** |
| `tags` | **Yes** |
| `models` | No — silently ignored |
| `fieldSchema` | No — silently ignored |
| `config` | No — silently ignored |
| `baseAnalyzerId` | No — silently ignored |
| `dynamicFieldSchema` | No — silently ignored |
| `processingLocation` | No — silently ignored |
| `knowledgeSources` | No — silently ignored |

## Workaround

To change create-only properties on an existing analyzer, use `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true`. This calls `PUT` (full resource replacement) instead of `PATCH`.

## Related

- #57215 — customer report of `UpdateAnalyzerAsync` not updating `models`

More details will be updated here.","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"ArcturusZhang"},"labels":["CodeGen","Provisioning"],"created_at":"2026-03-18T03:07:39Z","updated_at":"2026-03-18T03:07:54Z"},{"number":57184,"title":"Explore TypeForwarding Azure.Identity types to Azure.Core to enable per-library AddFooClient extensions","body":"## Problem

Individual SDK libraries (e.g., `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets`) need to provide `AddFooClient` DI extension methods that call `WithAzureCredential` to enable the full configuration and dependency injection experience described in #55491. Currently, `WithAzureCredential` and its supporting types (`ConfigurableCredential`, `ConfigurableCredentialCache`, `DefaultAzureCredentialOptions`) are defined in `Azure.Identity`. This means each SDK library would need to take a **new direct dependency on Azure.Identity** to offer these extensions — adding a dependency to every SDK package that doesn't currently have one.

## Proposed Approach to Explore

Since all SDK libraries already depend on `Azure.Core`, and `Azure.Identity` already depends on `Azure.Core`, we should explore whether we can use [TypeForwardedTo](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.runtime.compilerservices.typeforwardedtoattribute) to make the relevant types available through `Azure.Core` without requiring a direct `Azure.Identity` dependency.

The high-level idea:
1. **Move** the types needed by `WithAzureCredential` from `Azure.Identity` into `Azure.Core`
2. **Add** `[assembly: TypeForwardedTo(...)]` attributes in `Azure.Identity` pointing to the new locations in `Azure.Core`
3. SDK libraries can then reference these types through their existing `Azure.Core` dependency
4. **Existing consumers of Azure.Identity are unaffected** — the runtime follows type forwarding transparently, so no breaking change occurs

This should not break any existing users of `Azure.Identity` since:
- `TypeForwardedTo` is a well-established .NET mechanism for moving types across assemblies
- `Azure.Core` is already in every SDK's dependency chain
- Existing compiled code referencing these types via `Azure.Identity` will be redirected at runtime

## Types That May Need to Move

Based on the current `WithAzureCredential` implementation in `Azure.Identity/src/ConfigurationExtensions.cs`, the following types are referenced:

| Type | Current Location | Notes |
|------|-----------------|-------|
| `ConfigurableCredential` | Azure.Identity (currently internal) | Extends `TokenCredential`; visibility may need adjustment |
| `ConfigurableCredentialCache` | Azure.Identity | Static caching layer for credential reuse |
| `DefaultAzureCredentialOptions` | Azure.Identity | Options class for credential configuration |
| `WithAzureCredential` extensions | Azure.Identity (`ConfigurationExtensions`) | The extension methods on `ClientSettings` and `IClientBuilder` |

## Open Questions and Risks

- **Internal types**: `ConfigurableCredential` is currently `internal` — `TypeForwardedTo` only works with public types, so the API surface may need to be adjusted or an alternative abstraction introduced
- **Transitive dependency concerns**: `ConfigurableCredential` has deep dependencies on MSAL libraries, `DefaultAzureCredential`, `ChainedTokenCredential`, etc. — moving it directly to `Azure.Core` could pull in unwanted transitive dependencies unless the design is refactored (e.g., using an interface/abstraction in Azure.Core that Azure.Identity implements)
- **Alternative patterns**: Instead of moving concrete types, it may be more appropriate to define an abstraction or factory pattern in Azure.Core that Azure.Identity fulfills at runtime
- **Existing precedent**: `TypeForwardedTo` is rarely used in this repo today (only 1 instance found in `Azure.Messaging.EventGrid`), so this would establish a new pattern

## Goal

Enable SDK libraries to provide `AddFooClient` extension methods with full `WithAzureCredential` support **without adding a new direct dependency on Azure.Identity**. This issue tracks the investigation of TypeForwarding as the primary approach, but alternative solutions may emerge during exploration.","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"m-nash"},"labels":["Client","Azure.Core","Azure.Identity"],"comments":1,"created_at":"2026-03-17T18:53:04Z","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:04:52Z"},{"number":57183,"title":"[BUG] Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent NullReferenceException after successful streamed response finalization","body":"## Bug description
When using `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` through Microsoft Agent Framework streaming APIs, a chat run can complete successfully, return the full assistant answer, and then crash during stream finalization/disposal with a `System.NullReferenceException`.

The exception originates inside the Azure SDK telemetry/finalization path:
`Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(...)`

## Package and environment
- Package: `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`
- Package version: `1.0.0-preview.260311.1`
- App shape: .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework with Azure Foundry persistent agents
- OS: Windows
- Auth=[REDACTED] Streaming path: `AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(...)`

## Expected behavior
After the streamed answer completes, enumeration/disposal should finish cleanly.

## Actual behavior
The full assistant answer is returned to the caller, but the process crashes afterward during stream cleanup/final telemetry processing.

## Reproduction notes
1. Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.
2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.
3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.
4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.

## Minimal observed output
```text
assistant(stream)> 
[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...
```

The generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.

Relevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`

## Impact
- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.
- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.
- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.

## Workaround currently used
Application-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.

Please confirm whether this is a known bug in `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` preview and whether a null-safe fix should be added around `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` serialization / telemetry attribute generation.","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"brandonh-msft"},"labels":["Service Attention","Client","customer-reported","needs-team-attention","AI Agents"],"created_at":"2026-03-17T18:52:04Z","updated_at":"2026-03-19T03:29:58Z"},{"number":57156,"title":"[Azure.Provisioning.DataFactory] Design proper mapping for DataFactoryElement in provisioning library","body":"## Summary

The current Azure.Provisioning.DataFactory generator maps `DataFactoryElement` (from the `Azure.Core.Expressions.DataFactory` package) to `BicepValue` by unwrapping the generic type argument. This mapping is incorrect and needs a proper design.

## Background

`DataFactoryElement` is a special type in the Azure Data Factory SDK that represents values which can be one of:
- A **literal value** of type `T`
- A **Data Factory expression** (e.g., `@pipeline().parameters.myParam`)
- A **secret reference** (Azure Key Vault)

When we generate the provisioning library, the generator's `ResolveExternalGenericType` hook simply strips the `DataFactoryElement` wrapper and uses `T` directly, which then becomes `BicepValue` in the generated code. This loses the ability to express Data Factory expressions and secret references in the Bicep output.

## Current Behavior

`DataFactoryElement` -> generator unwraps to `string` -> becomes `BicepValue`

This means a property like `FilterActivity.Condition` (which is `DataFactoryElement`) becomes `BicepValue` and can only hold a literal boolean value - it cannot represent a Data Factory expression like `@equals(1, 1)`.

## Expected Behavior

We need a proper design that preserves the semantics of `DataFactoryElement` in Bicep. Options to consider:

1. **Map to `BicepValue`** - allows arbitrary JSON including expression objects, but loses type safety
2. **Create a custom `BicepDataFactoryExpression`** - a provisioning-specific type that can serialize to either a literal `T` or a `{ value, type }` expression object
3. **Map to `BicepValue`** always - since Data Factory expressions are string-based, always emit as string and let ADF runtime handle type coercion
4. **Other approaches** - open to discussion

## Affected Properties

Hundreds of properties across the Data Factory provisioning library use `DataFactoryElement` in the management SDK, including:
- `FilterActivity.Condition` (`DataFactoryElement`)
- `FilterActivity.Items` (`DataFactoryElement>>`)
- `CopyActivity.EnableStaging` (`DataFactoryElement`)
- Many linked service connection properties

## Related

- PR #57127 - Initial Azure.Provisioning.DataFactory library
- `Azure.Core.Expressions.DataFactory` package - defines `DataFactoryElement`
- Generator code: `sdk/provisioning/Generator/src/Specifications/DataFactorySpecification.cs`","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"ArcturusZhang"},"labels":["Data Factory","Provisioning"],"created_at":"2026-03-17T06:31:03Z","updated_at":"2026-03-17T15:50:43Z"},{"number":57141,"title":"Create MCP server for Azure.GeneratorAgent","body":"### Library name

We should hook this tool into existing code fixers if the request is applicable. For example, if the request includes build output that includes analyzer errors","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"m-redding"},"labels":["Client","SDK Dev Experience"],"created_at":"2026-03-16T18:03:55Z","updated_at":"2026-03-16T18:52:12Z"},{"number":57107,"title":"[MPG Emitter] Support markAsNonResource client option to exclude ARM resource models from provider schema","body":"## Background

During Swagger-to-TypeSpec migration of **Azure.ResourceManager.EventHubs**, we discovered a feature gap: some ARM resource models (e.g. `NetworkSecurityPerimeterConfiguration`) were treated as **non-resources** in the old Swagger-based SDK via the `request-path-is-non-resource` directive, but are promoted to full ARM resources in the TypeSpec definition.

This causes backward-incompatible API surface changes — operations that belonged to the parent resource (e.g. `EventHubsNamespaceResource`) now appear on a new child resource type that didn't exist in the prior SDK.

## Feature

Add support for the `markAsNonResource` client option in the MPG C# emitter, allowing TypeSpec authors to annotate a resource model so the emitter excludes it from the ARM provider schema.

### Usage in TypeSpec

```tsp
@@clientOption(NetworkSecurityPerimeterConfiguration, "markAsNonResource", true, "csharp");
```

### Behavior

- The emitter reads `@clientOption("markAsNonResource", true)` via TCGC's `getClientOptions()` API
- Matching resource entries are removed from `ArmProviderSchema`
- Their operations become `NonResourceMethod[]` and are reassigned to the nearest parent resource via path prefix matching
- If a marked resource is a **parent** of another resource, a warning is emitted and the resource is kept (child-resource reparenting is not yet supported)

### Design Principles

- **Parsing is centralized**: all `@clientOption` decorators are parsed into a `Map` early in the pipeline
- **Application is per-option**: each option (e.g. `markAsNonResource`) has its own exported apply function, called at the appropriate pipeline phase — extensible for future client options
- **Generator is unaware**: the generator faithfully follows the emitter's TspCodeModel output. It never knows a resource was marked as non-resource

## Implementation

Branch: `feature/mark-as-non-resource`

### Files Changed

- **`emitter/src/client-option-processor.ts`** (new) — core logic: `parseResourceClientOptions()`, `applyMarkAsNonResource()`
- **`emitter/src/resource-detection.ts`** (modified) — integration in `updateClients()`
- **`emitter/test/mark-as-non-resource.test.ts`** (new) — 4 test cases:
  1. Child resource removal with operation reassignment
  2. Multi-path resource removal (same model at multiple paths)
  3. Parent resource guard (warning + keep)
  4. No-op when no `@clientOption` is present

### Test Results

All 67 emitter tests pass (0 regressions across 4 test files).

## Limitations

- Marking a resource that is a **parent** of another resource is not supported yet (warning emitted, resource kept)
- Requires TCGC >= 0.65.x for `getClientOptions()` API","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"haiyuazhang"},"labels":["Mgmt","CodeGen"],"created_at":"2026-03-15T07:22:44Z","updated_at":"2026-03-15T17:51:06Z"},{"number":57091,"title":""Explore the client APIs" 404s on the Azure Application Insights libraries for .NET page","body":"### Type of issue

* Service: **application-insights**","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"winstliu"},"labels":["Service Attention","Client","customer-reported","needs-team-attention","Monitor - ApplicationInsights"],"comments":2,"created_at":"2026-03-13T19:04:47Z","updated_at":"2026-03-13T19:14:06Z"},{"number":57073,"title":"[Codegen Bug] MPG generator incorrectly assigns resourceScope 'Subscription' instead of 'Tenant' for TenantSupportTicket in tspCodeModel.json","body":"## Bug Description

The MPG (Management Plane Generator) codegen incorrectly assigns `resourceScope: "Subscription"` to the `TenantSupportTicket` resource in the generated `tspCodeModel.json`, when it should be `"Tenant"`.

## Root Cause

In the TypeSpec spec ([SupportTicketDetails.tsp](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/blob/[REDACTED]/specification/support/resource-manager/Microsoft.Support/Support/SupportTicketDetails.tsp)), the `SupportTicketDetails` model is marked `@subscriptionResource` and shared by two operation interfaces with different scopes:

1. **`SupportTickets`** — subscription-scoped (includes `SubscriptionIdParameter`)
2. **`SupportTicketsNoSubscription`** — tenant-scoped (no `SubscriptionIdParameter`)

In `client.tsp`, these are renamed:
```tsp
@@clientName(SupportTickets, "SubscriptionSupportTicket", "csharp");
@@clientName(SupportTicketsNoSubscription, "TenantSupportTicket", "csharp");
```

## Evidence in tspCodeModel.json

### ✅ SubscriptionSupportTicket — Correct (Subscription scope)
```json
{
  "resourceModelId": "Microsoft.Support.SupportTicketDetails",
  "resourceIdPattern": "/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/providers/Microsoft.Support/supportTickets/{supportTicketName}",
  "resourceType": "Microsoft.Support/supportTickets",
  "methods": [
    { "methodId": "Microsoft.Support.SupportTickets.create", "operationScope": "Subscription" },
    { "methodId": "Microsoft.Support.SupportTickets.get", "operationScope": "Subscription" },
    { "methodId": "Microsoft.Support.SupportTickets.update", "operationScope": "Subscription" },
    { "methodId": "Microsoft.Support.SupportTickets.list", "operationScope": "Subscription" }
  ],
  "resourceScope": "Subscription",   ✅ Correct
  "resourceName": "SubscriptionSupportTicket"
}
```

### ❌ TenantSupportTicket — Wrong scope (should be Tenant)
```json
{
  "resourceModelId": "Microsoft.Support.SupportTicketDetails",
  "resourceIdPattern": "/providers/Microsoft.Support/supportTickets/{supportTicketName}",
  "resourceType": "Microsoft.Support/supportTickets",
  "methods": [
    { "methodId": "Microsoft.Support.SupportTicketsNoSubscription.create", "operationScope": "Tenant" },
    { "methodId": "Microsoft.Support.SupportTicketsNoSubscription.get", "operationScope": "Tenant" },
    { "methodId": "Microsoft.Support.SupportTicketsNoSubscription.update", "operationScope": "Tenant" },
    { "methodId": "Microsoft.Support.SupportTicketsNoSubscription.list", "operationScope": "Tenant" }
  ],
  "resourceScope": "Subscription",   ❌ BUG: Should be "Tenant"
  "resourceName": "TenantSupportTicket"
}
```

**Note:** All methods under `TenantSupportTicket` have `"operationScope": "Tenant"` and use tenant-level paths (`/providers/Microsoft.Support/...` without `{subscriptionId}`), but the top-level `resourceScope` is incorrectly set to `"Subscription"`.

For comparison, another tenant-scoped resource `SupportTicketNoSubCommunication` in the same tspCodeModel.json correctly has `"resourceScope": "Tenant"`.

## Impact on Generated SDK Code

Due to the incorrect `resourceScope`, the generator produces incorrect code for `TenantSupportTicketCollection`:

1. **Wrong `ValidateResourceId`**: The generated code only validates against `SubscriptionResource.ResourceType`, rejecting `TenantResource.ResourceType` — even though tenant-level access is a valid scenario.

2. **Wrong XML doc comments**: The generated class doc says:
   > Each TenantSupportTicketResource in the collection will belong to the same instance of **SubscriptionResource**.

   This is incorrect for a tenant-scoped resource.

3. **Wrong parent scope placement**: `GetTenantSupportTickets()` is generated on `MockableSupportSubscriptionResource` instead of being also available on `MockableSupportTenantResource` as a collection getter.

### SDK Workarounds Required

These issues required custom code in `TenantSupportTicketCollection.cs` to:
- Override `ValidateResourceId` to accept both `SubscriptionResource.ResourceType` and `TenantResource.ResourceType`
- Restore `GetAll`/`GetAllAsync` methods and `IEnumerable`/`IAsyncEnumerable` interfaces for backward compatibility

## Expected Behavior

The `TenantSupportTicket` resource entry in `tspCodeModel.json` should have:
```json
"resourceScope": "Tenant"
```

This would cause the generator to:
- Generate `ValidateResourceId` checking against `TenantResource.ResourceType`
- Generate correct doc comments referencing `TenantResource`
- Place the collection under `TenantResource` scope

## Spec Reference

- **Spec commit**: `[REDACTED]`
- **Spec file**: `specification/support/resource-manager/Microsoft.Support/Support/SupportTicketDetails.tsp`
- **Emitter**: `eng/azure-typespec-http-client-csharp-mgmt-emitter-package.json`

## Reproduction

Generate the Support SDK from the TypeSpec spec and inspect the `tspCodeModel.json` for the `TenantSupportTicket` resource entry.

## Affected SDK

`sdk/support/Azure.ResourceManager.Support`","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"mzhongl524"},"labels":["Mgmt","CodeGen"],"comments":1,"created_at":"2026-03-13T09:55:56Z","updated_at":"2026-03-16T02:46:52Z"},{"number":57058,"title":"[Mgmt Generator Bug] Derived type constructor passes wrong type to flattened base constructor","body":"## Bug Description

When a derived model inherits from a flattened base type, the generated public constructor passes the unflattenable inner model type (`DataConnectorDataTypeCommon`) to the base constructor, but the base public constructor expects the flattened convenience type (`SecurityInsightsDataTypeConnectionState?`). This causes CS1503 build errors.

## Impact

**1 file** affected in SecurityInsights migration (#56944), but likely affects any similar inheritance pattern with flattened properties.

## How to Reproduce

1. Clone `azure-sdk-for-net` and `azure-rest-api-specs`
2. Checkout branch `securityinsights-typespec-migration` in azure-sdk-for-net
3. Checkout branch `securityinsights-csharp-migration` in azure-rest-api-specs
4. Run code generation:
```bash
cd sdk/securityinsights/Azure.ResourceManager.SecurityInsights/src
dotnet build /t:GenerateCode /p:LocalSpecRepo=/specification/securityinsights/resource-manager/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/SecurityInsights
```
5. Build: `dotnet build`
6. Observe CS1503 in `Models/MCASDataConnectorDataTypes.cs`

## Root Cause

`MCASDataConnectorDataTypes` extends `SecurityInsightsAlertsDataTypeOfDataConnector`. The base type has a flattened public constructor that takes `SecurityInsightsDataTypeConnectionState?`, but the derived type's generated constructor passes the unflattened inner type `DataConnectorDataTypeCommon`.

**Example:** `Models/MCASDataConnectorDataTypes.cs` (line 20):
```csharp
public partial class MCASDataConnectorDataTypes : SecurityInsightsAlertsDataTypeOfDataConnector
{
    public MCASDataConnectorDataTypes(DataConnectorDataTypeCommon alerts) : base(alerts)  // BUG
```

Base type public constructor:
```csharp
public SecurityInsightsAlertsDataTypeOfDataConnector(SecurityInsightsDataTypeConnectionState? alertsState)
```

Types involved:
- **Passed:** `DataConnectorDataTypeCommon` (the full inner model)
- **Expected:** `SecurityInsightsDataTypeConnectionState?` (the flattened convenience parameter)

## Expected Behavior

The derived constructor should either:
- Accept the flattened convenience parameter type and pass it through, or
- Accept the inner model and extract the flattened property to pass to base

## Affected Files

- `Models/MCASDataConnectorDataTypes.cs`

Also related: `TiTaxiiDataConnectorDataTypesTaxiiClient.cs` has AZC0005/AZC0007 errors (missing public/protected constructors), which may share the same root cause.
","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"live1206"},"labels":["Mgmt","CodeGen"],"comments":1,"created_at":"2026-03-13T07:16:09Z","updated_at":"2026-03-13T19:12:51Z"},{"number":57057,"title":"[Mgmt Generator Bug] Collection emits IEnumerable boilerplate for non-pageable list operation","body":"## Bug Description

The generator emits standard `IEnumerable.GetEnumerator()` and `IAsyncEnumerable.GetAsyncEnumerator()` implementations that call `GetAll().GetEnumerator()`, but `GetAll()` returns `Response` (a single-response list operation) rather than a pageable collection, causing CS1061 errors.

## Impact

**3 errors** in SecurityInsights migration (#56944).

## How to Reproduce

1. Clone `azure-sdk-for-net` and `azure-rest-api-specs`
2. Checkout branch `securityinsights-typespec-migration` in azure-sdk-for-net
3. Checkout branch `securityinsights-csharp-migration` in azure-rest-api-specs
4. Run code generation:
```bash
cd sdk/securityinsights/Azure.ResourceManager.SecurityInsights/src
dotnet build /t:GenerateCode /p:LocalSpecRepo=/specification/securityinsights/resource-manager/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/SecurityInsights
```
5. Build: `dotnet build`
6. Observe CS1061 errors in `SecurityInsightsSentinelOnboardingStateCollection.cs`

## Root Cause

The `SentinelOnboardingStates.list` operation returns a single non-paginated list (`SentinelOnboardingStatesList`), not a pageable collection. The generator still emits the standard collection `IEnumerable`/`IAsyncEnumerable` boilerplate that expects `GetAll()` to return an enumerable.

**Example:** `SecurityInsightsSentinelOnboardingStateCollection.cs`:
```csharp
// GetAll() returns Response, NOT a pageable
public virtual Response GetAll(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)

// But the generator emits:
IEnumerator IEnumerable<...>.GetEnumerator()
{
    return GetAll().GetEnumerator();  // BUG: Response has no GetEnumerator()
}

IAsyncEnumerator<...> IAsyncEnumerable<...>.GetAsyncEnumerator(CancellationToken cancellationToken)
{
    return GetAllAsync(...).GetAsyncEnumerator(cancellationToken);  // BUG: Task> has no GetAsyncEnumerator()
}
```

## Expected Behavior

For non-pageable list operations, the generator should either:
- Not emit the `IEnumerable`/`IAsyncEnumerable` interface implementations, or
- Unwrap the response value to get the list and iterate over it (e.g., `GetAll().Value.Value.GetEnumerator()`)

## Affected Files

- `SecurityInsightsSentinelOnboardingStateCollection.cs` (lines 595, 600, 606)
","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"live1206"},"labels":["Mgmt","CodeGen"],"comments":1,"created_at":"2026-03-13T07:15:44Z","updated_at":"2026-03-13T19:12:50Z"},{"number":57056,"title":"[Mgmt Generator Bug] Resource serialization instantiates abstract base type","body":"## Bug Description

The generator emits `new AbstractType()` in resource serialization helpers for polymorphic/discriminated base types. Since these types are `abstract`, this causes CS0144 build errors.

## Impact

**6 files** affected in SecurityInsights migration (#56944).

## How to Reproduce

1. Clone `azure-sdk-for-net` and `azure-rest-api-specs`
2. Checkout branch `securityinsights-typespec-migration` in azure-sdk-for-net
3. Checkout branch `securityinsights-csharp-migration` in azure-rest-api-specs
4. Run code generation:
```bash
cd sdk/securityinsights/Azure.ResourceManager.SecurityInsights/src
dotnet build /t:GenerateCode /p:LocalSpecRepo=/specification/securityinsights/resource-manager/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/SecurityInsights
```
5. Build: `dotnet build`
6. Observe CS0144 errors: `Cannot create an instance of the abstract type or interface`

## Root Cause

The generator creates a static deserialization instance field pattern for resource serialization. For polymorphic base types (discriminated unions), it tries to instantiate the abstract base class directly instead of using the static deserializer.

**Example:** `SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticResource.Serialization.cs` (line 19):
```csharp
private static IJsonModel DataDeserializationInstance 
    => s_dataDeserializationInstance ??= new SecurityInsights.SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticData();  // BUG: abstract type
```

But `SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticData` is declared as:
```csharp
public abstract partial class SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticData : ResourceData
```

## Expected Behavior

For abstract/polymorphic base types, the generator should use the base type's static `Deserialize` method or a non-abstract deserializer pattern instead of `new AbstractType()`.

## Affected Files

- `SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticResource.Serialization.cs` — `SecurityInsightsBillingStatisticData`
- `SecurityInsightsDataConnectorDefinitionResource.Serialization.cs` — `SecurityInsightsDataConnectorDefinitionData`
- `SecurityInsightsEntityQueryResource.Serialization.cs` — `SecurityInsightsEntityQueryData`
- `SecurityInsightsEntityQueryTemplateResource.Serialization.cs` — `SecurityInsightsEntityQueryTemplateData`
- `SecurityInsightsEntityResource.Serialization.cs` — `SecurityInsightsEntity`
- `SecurityInsightsSettingsResource.Serialization.cs` — `SecurityInsightsSettingsData`
","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"live1206"},"labels":["Mgmt","CodeGen"],"comments":1,"created_at":"2026-03-13T07:15:21Z","updated_at":"2026-03-13T19:12:49Z"},{"number":57055,"title":"[Mgmt Generator Bug] Generated code calls BinaryData.FromResponse which does not exist","body":"## Bug Description

The generator emits `BinaryData.FromResponse(result)` in resource operation methods, but `BinaryData` does not have a static `FromResponse` method. This causes CS0117 build errors.

## Impact

**4 files** affected in SecurityInsights migration (#56944).

## How to Reproduce

1. Clone `azure-sdk-for-net` and `azure-rest-api-specs`
2. Checkout branch `securityinsights-typespec-migration` in azure-sdk-for-net
3. Checkout branch `securityinsights-csharp-migration` in azure-rest-api-specs
4. Run code generation:
```bash
cd sdk/securityinsights/Azure.ResourceManager.SecurityInsights/src
dotnet build /t:GenerateCode /p:LocalSpecRepo=/specification/securityinsights/resource-manager/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/SecurityInsights
```
5. Build: `dotnet build`
6. Observe CS0117 errors: `BinaryData does not contain a definition for FromResponse`

## Root Cause

The generator treats `BinaryData` like a generated model type and emits a model-style `FromResponse` helper call. But `BinaryData` is a framework type that does not have this static method.

**Example:** `SecurityInsightsAutomationRuleResource.cs` (line 226):
```csharp
Response result = await Pipeline.ProcessMessageAsync(message, context).ConfigureAwait(false);
Response response = Response.FromValue(BinaryData.FromResponse(result), result);  // BUG
```

## Expected Behavior

Should use `result.Content` instead:
```csharp
Response response = Response.FromValue(result.Content, result);
```

## Affected Files

- `SecurityInsightsAutomationRuleResource.cs` (lines 226, 278)
- `SecurityInsightsIncidentResource.cs` (lines 617, 666)

These are resource types whose operations return `BinaryData` (e.g., delete operations with response bodies).
","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"live1206"},"labels":["Service Attention","Mgmt","CodeGen"],"comments":1,"created_at":"2026-03-13T07:14:57Z","updated_at":"2026-03-13T19:13:03Z"},{"number":57054,"title":"[Mgmt Generator Bug] Serialization uses undeclared variable additionalBinaryDataProperties0","body":"## Bug Description

In generated serialization code for flattened discriminated types, the deserialization method declares a variable named `additionalBinaryDataProperties` but the catch-all branch references `additionalBinaryDataProperties0` (with trailing `0`), causing CS0103 build errors.

## Impact

**89 files** affected in SecurityInsights migration (#56944). This blocks the entire migration.

## How to Reproduce

1. Clone `azure-sdk-for-net` and `azure-rest-api-specs`
2. Checkout branch `securityinsights-typespec-migration` in azure-sdk-for-net
3. Checkout branch `securityinsights-csharp-migration` in azure-rest-api-specs
4. Run code generation:
```bash
cd sdk/securityinsights/Azure.ResourceManager.SecurityInsights/src
dotnet build /t:GenerateCode /p:LocalSpecRepo=/specification/securityinsights/resource-manager/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/SecurityInsights
```
5. Build: `dotnet build`
6. Observe CS0103 errors in 89 `.Serialization.cs` files

## Root Cause

The bug occurs in types that have `@@Legacy.flattenProperty` in `back-compatible.tsp`. When the generator flattens a `properties` bag into the parent type, the serialization code generator appends a `0` suffix to the `additionalBinaryDataProperties` variable reference but not to its declaration.

**Example:** `Models/AATPDataConnector.Serialization.cs`

Declaration (line 117):
```csharp
IDictionary additionalBinaryDataProperties = new ChangeTrackingDictionary();
```

Usage (line 176) — **BUG**: `0` suffix added:
```csharp
additionalBinaryDataProperties0.Add(prop.Name, BinaryData.FromString(prop.Value.GetRawText()));
```

Constructor call (line 184) — no suffix:
```csharp
return new AATPDataConnector(id, name, resourceType, systemData, additionalBinaryDataProperties, kind, eTag, properties);
```

## Expected Behavior

The variable reference should match the declaration: `additionalBinaryDataProperties` (no `0` suffix).

## Affected Files (sample)

- `AATPDataConnector.Serialization.cs`
- `SecurityInsightsAccountEntity.Serialization.cs`
- `SecurityInsightsAadDataConnector.Serialization.cs`
- `MLBehaviorAnalyticsAlertRule.Serialization.cs`
- `SecurityInsightsFusionAlertRule.Serialization.cs`
- ... and 84 more

All affected types have `@@Legacy.flattenProperty(TypeName.properties)` in `back-compatible.tsp`.
","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"live1206"},"labels":["Mgmt","CodeGen"],"comments":1,"created_at":"2026-03-13T07:14:37Z","updated_at":"2026-03-13T19:13:02Z"},{"number":57036,"title":"[QUERY] - Azure Document Intelligence future?","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.DocumentIntelligence 1.0.0

### Query/Question

Hey guys, I'm a bit confused with the direction Azure Document Intelligence is taking.

The latest API version is 2024-11-30 (GA). Which, by recent AI standards is quite old. Additionally, I cannot find any information what the underlying model version is, but out of experience - it doesn't seem to have changed since.

I can see that Azure Content Understanding is being pushed instead and is the only thing with a latest update, but that also seems to have a different workflow.

So, a couple of questions:
- What's the expectation here? Is Document Intelligence going to see any future updates?
- Should we be bracing for a switch instead?
- How would an invoice Document Intelligence work-flow which relies on QueryFields and key-value field pairs look like in Content Understanding? An Invoice request followed by Document Fields request?
- Would there ever be the option to instruct Content Understanding to look for specific fields (similar to Query Fields in Document Intelligence)?

### Environment

Update the Storage Blobs package to use TypeSpec instead of autorest

# Principles

- This is primarily an exercise to migrate the tool chain. This is NOT an exercise to
  - Improve the convenience layer to latest guidelines
  - Ask for new complex features in TypeSpec in order to remove already written manual convenience layer. For instance, paging in Storage couldn't be generated with autorest and was manually written. We don't plan to make TypeSpec support Storage paging out of the box, we'll just keep for now the convenience layer that exists. Easy TypeSpec update can be considered, as long as this doesn't impact the timeline in an unhealthy way.
- As this is an internal toolchain update, there should be no reason to update tests or re-record tests. It's actually a great regression testing to confirm that we're able to change the internal layers of Storage, without having to touch the test folder at all
- No breaking changes are allowed (APIView shouldn't even realize there was a change)

# Spec

We owned this branch, and should push all tspconfig and client.tsp updates directly there (no review needed yet):
https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/tree/feature/file-tsp/specification/storage/data-plane/FileStorage

`tsplocation` should target this branch for now

More details and update on the [loop paage](https://microsoft.sharepoint.com/:fl:/s/4b8cd56b-6d40-4ab3-bea5-1744d4d73cdf/IQCVtwVSrfJ3Q6ZDSlY42ayEAYd_nPaeWNWHG6x7S8PjnXs?e=gGUlYa&nav=cz0lMkZzaXRlcyUyRjRiOGNkNTZiLTZkNDAtNGFiMy1iZWE1LTE3NDRkNGQ3M2NkZiZkPWIlMjFvcWVpNUNkU2owU0JoMUJuLVFQTjJxSzBYVnBPaWFGUGhzZ1BUaEdSTnRvNDNjM1JLMU05UXA0T1ZxZlV3b2VpJmY9MDFVUVdFWVBNVlc0Q1ZGTFBTTzVCMk1RMktLWTROVExFRSZjPSUyRiZhPUxvb3BBcHAmcD0lNDBmbHVpZHglMkZsb29wLXBhZ2UtY29udGFpbmVy)","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"lmazuel"},"labels":["Storage","Client","CodeGen","Epic"],"comments":1,"created_at":"2026-03-12T20:25:56Z","updated_at":"2026-03-12T20:33:44Z"},{"number":57026,"title":"Copy From SharePoint Online to Azure Blob","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.Security.KeyVault.*

### Please describe the feature.

### Feature request

Please add support for Key Vault REST API version `2026-02-01` in the `Azure.Security.KeyVault.*` client libraries (`Secrets`, `Keys`, and `Certificates`).

### Background

Microsoft Learn now documents the Key Vault access control default change for API version `2026-02-01` and later.  
According to the documentation, new vaults created with `2026-02-01` or later default to Azure RBAC, and older API versions are planned to be retired by **2027-02-27**.

At the moment, the current `main` branch of the .NET Key Vault client libraries still appears to use `2025-07-01` as the latest supported service version, for example:

- `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets.SecretClientOptions`
- `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Keys.KeyClientOptions`
- `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Certificates.CertificateClientOptions`

### Why this matters

Applications and tooling that need to align with the new Key Vault API behavior cannot explicitly target `2026-02-01` yet via the .NET SDK.

Support for `2026-02-01` would help with:
- validating behavior against the new default access control model
- preparing applications before older API versions are retired
- avoiding custom or workaround-based REST calls outside the SDK

### Requested change

Please add support for Key Vault REST API version `2026-02-01` to the relevant `Azure.Security.KeyVault.*` libraries and expose it through the corresponding client options.

If full support is not planned yet, could you share the expected timeline / ETA or whether there is an existing tracking issue or PR for this work?

### References

- [Microsoft Learn: “Prepare for Key Vault API versions 2026-02-01 and later”](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/key-vault/general/access-control-default?tabs=azure-cli)","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"natori-tatsuya"},"labels":["KeyVault","Service Attention","Client","customer-reported","feature-request","needs-team-attention"],"comments":2,"created_at":"2026-03-12T03:15:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-12T15:34:42Z"},{"number":56996,"title":"[MPG Generator] HEAD operations have plural resourceName in tspCodeModel, causing methods to be placed on parent resource instead of self","body":"## Problem Description

When migrating from AutoRest (Swagger) to the TypeSpec MPG generator, **HEAD operations** are generated differently, causing **breaking changes** for GA packages.

This was discovered during the `Azure.ResourceManager.ApiCenter` migration from AutoRest to TypeSpec, requiring custom backward-compatibility shim code to preserve the 1.0.0 GA public API surface.

## Root Cause

Verified in `tspCodeModel.json`: **all HEAD operations have `resourceName` set to the plural form (interface name)** instead of the singular form (resource name) that CRUD operations use.

For example:
- HEAD operation: `"resourceName": "MetadataSchemas"` ❌ (plural/interface name)
- CRUD operations (get, createOrUpdate, delete): `"resourceName": "MetadataSchema"` ✅ (singular/resource name)

This mismatch causes the C# MPG generator to fail to match the HEAD operation to its resource class, resulting in:
1. **Method placement**: Moved from the resource itself to the parent resource
2. **Return type**: Changed from `Response` to `Response` (void)

The issue originates in the **TCGC layer**, not the C# generator itself. HEAD operations defined via raw `@head` decorator with `ResourceInstanceParameters` don't go through the standard ARM operation templates, so TCGC assigns them the interface name (plural) as `resourceName` instead of the resource name (singular).

## Expected Behavior (AutoRest/Swagger)

In the old AutoRest-generated code, HEAD operations were generated as **self-check methods on the resource itself**, returning `Response`:

```csharp
// On ApiCenterApiDefinitionResource itself
public virtual async Task> HeadAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)
public virtual Response Head(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)
```

## Actual Behavior (TypeSpec MPG Generator)

After migration, the TypeSpec MPG generator places HEAD operations on the **parent resource** with a child name parameter, returning `Response` (not `Response`):

```csharp
// On ApiCenterApiVersionResource (parent) instead of ApiCenterApiDefinitionResource (self)
public virtual async Task HeadAsync(string definitionName, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)
public virtual Response Head(string definitionName, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)
```

## tspCodeModel.json Evidence

All 8 HEAD operations show the same pattern — plural `resourceName` (interface name) instead of singular (resource name):

| HEAD Operation | `resourceName` (actual) | Expected (CRUD ops use) | Path |
|---|---|---|---|
| `HeadMetadataSchema` | `MetadataSchemas` ❌ | `MetadataSchema` | `.../metadataSchemas/{metadataSchemaName}` |
| `HeadWorkspace` | `Workspaces` ❌ | `Workspace` | `.../workspaces/{workspaceName}` |
| `HeadApi` | `Apis` ❌ | `Api` | `.../apis/{apiName}` |
| `HeadApiVersion` | `ApiVersions` ❌ | `ApiVersion` | `.../versions/{versionName}` |
| `head` (ApiDefinition) | `ApiDefinitions` ❌ | `ApiDefinition` | `.../definitions/{definitionName}` |
| `HeadApiSource` | `ApiSources` ❌ | `ApiSource` | `.../apiSources/{apiSourceName}` |
| `HeadDeployment` | `Deployments` ❌ | `Deployment` | `.../deployments/{deploymentName}` |
| `HeadEnvironment` | `Environments` ❌ | `Environment` | `.../environments/{environmentName}` |

## TypeSpec Definition

The TypeSpec defines HEAD as a standard operation on the resource interface using `ResourceInstanceParameters`:

```typespec
@armResourceOperations
interface ApiDefinitions {
  @doc("Checks if specified API definition exists.")
  @head
  head(
    ...ResourceInstanceParameters<
      ApiDefinition,
      Foundations.BaseParameters
    >,
  ): OkResponse | ErrorResponse;
}
```

## Suggested Fix

The fix should be in the **TCGC layer**: when processing HEAD operations defined with `ResourceInstanceParameters`, TCGC should use the singular resource name `T` (e.g., `MetadataSchema`) as `resourceName`, not the plural interface name (e.g., `MetadataSchemas`). This would align HEAD operations with how CRUD operations are handled.

---

> **Note**: The original issue also included "ArmResourceActionAsync with typed response renames the method" (ImportSpecification → ImportSpecificationWithResult). That was determined to be **not a generator bug** — the rename is explicitly set in the spec's `client.tsp` via `@@clientName(ApiDefinitions.importSpecification, "ImportSpecificationWithResult", "csharp")`. The fix is to update the spec's `client.tsp`.
","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"mzhongl524"},"labels":["Mgmt","CodeGen"],"comments":3,"created_at":"2026-03-12T02:13:07Z","updated_at":"2026-03-13T02:44:15Z"},{"number":56986,"title":"Azure.Identity: Support programmatic Transport and Policy customization for ConfigurableCredential and ChainedTokenCredential","body":"Currently, credentials created via `IConfiguration` (through `ConfigurableCredential` and `ChainedTokenCredential`) are constructed entirely from config — there is no hook point for developers to programmatically set `Transport` or add `HttpPipelinePolicy` instances to credential options.

This is by design for the config-driven creation path, but creates a gap for scenarios where developers need to:

1. **Override Transport** — e.g., inject a custom `HttpClientTransport` backed by `IHttpClientFactory`, or use a corporate proxy transport
2. **Add policies** — e.g., logging policies, telemetry policies, or custom retry policies that can't be expressed as config values
3. **Apply to chained credentials** — each source in a `ChainedTokenCredential` has its own options, so customizations need to reach individual credential instances inside the chain

### Current Behavior

- `ClientOptions(IConfigurationSection)` always creates a default transport via `HttpPipelineTransport.Create()` — config has no transport binding
- `HttpPipelineTransport` is abstract and cannot be instantiated from config
- `HttpPipelinePolicy` instances are added via `AddPolicy()` — a programmatic-only API
- `ChainedTokenCredentialFactory` creates credentials directly from `DefaultAzureCredentialOptions` source elements with no parent propagation of Transport or policies

### Desired Behavior

Provide a mechanism for developers to customize Transport and/or add policies to credentials created from configuration. This should work for:

- Single credentials (e.g., `CredentialSource: "AzureCliCredential"`)
- `ChainedTokenCredential` with multiple sources

### Design Considerations

- Should this be a callback/delegate on `DefaultAzureCredentialOptions` that receives each credential's options before construction?
- Should there be a post-config `Action` that applies to all credentials in the chain?
- How does this interact with DI — should `IHttpClientFactory` integration be a first-class pattern?
- `Transport` is not serializable to JSON, so this is inherently a code + config hybrid scenario","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"m-nash"},"labels":["Client","Azure.Identity","feature-request"],"comments":2,"created_at":"2026-03-11T21:37:33Z","updated_at":"2026-03-11T22:26:00Z"},{"number":56965,"title":"[BUG] Keyvault Keys from mHSM are returning Null Encoded Policies","body":"### Library name and version

net8.0","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"ncamillucci"},"labels":["KeyVault","Service Attention","Client","question","needs-team-attention"],"comments":3,"created_at":"2026-03-11T11:16:30Z","updated_at":"2026-03-18T17:02:26Z"},{"number":56961,"title":"[Mgmt Generator] Read-only models with @@access(Access.public) generate incomplete code requiring  workaround","body":"## Bug Description

When a TypeSpec read-only model has `@@access(Access.public, "csharp")` applied, the management plane generator (`http-client-csharp-mgmt`) produces incomplete/incorrect generated code, requiring SDK authors to use `` in the `.csproj` and provide full custom replacements for both the model and its serialization.

## Reproduction

Found during the `Azure.ResourceManager.Hci` migration to TypeSpec ([PR #56586](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56586)).

The `ExtensionProfile` model is a read-only model (only has getter properties). Despite having `@@access(Access.public, "csharp")` in `client.tsp`, the generated code is incomplete — requiring the following workaround in the `.csproj`:

```xml


```

Plus two custom replacement files:
- `src/Custom/ExtensionProfile.cs` — constructors, properties, raw data field
- `src/Custom/ExtensionProfile.Serialization.cs` — full `IJsonModel` / `IPersistableModel` implementation

The model factory (`ArmHciModelFactory`) generates a public method that takes `ExtensionProfile` as a parameter, which causes **CS0051** (inconsistent accessibility) if the generator makes the model `internal`.

## Expected Behavior

When `@@access(Access.public, "csharp")` is set on a read-only model:

1. The generator should produce the **complete** model class (public, with constructors, properties, and the raw data field)
2. The generator should produce the **complete** serialization implementation
3. No `` workaround should be needed
4. The model factory should be able to reference the model without accessibility issues

## Actual Behavior

The generator's usage analysis overrides or interferes with the `@@access` decorator for read-only models, resulting in incomplete generated code that cannot be used directly.

## Affected Files

- **Generator**: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`
- **Affected SDK**: `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci/`
- **Workaround files**:
  - `src/Custom/ExtensionProfile.cs`
  - `src/Custom/ExtensionProfile.Serialization.cs`
  - `.csproj` `` entries

## Impact

Any TypeSpec-based management SDK migration that has read-only models exposed via model factory methods may hit this issue. SDK authors must manually maintain custom model + serialization files, which is error-prone and creates maintenance burden.","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"live1206"},"labels":["Mgmt","bug","CodeGen"],"comments":2,"created_at":"2026-03-11T09:34:19Z","updated_at":"2026-03-11T15:26:22Z"},{"number":56956,"title":"** MPG migration -
