TypeForwarding Azure.Identity types to Azure.Core to enable per-library AddFooClient extensions","body":"## Problem

Individual SDK libraries (e.g., `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets`) need to provide `AddFooClient` DI extension methods that call `WithAzureCredential` to enable the full configuration and dependency injection experience described in #55491. Currently, `WithAzureCredential` and its supporting types (`ConfigurableCredential`, `ConfigurableCredentialCache`, `DefaultAzureCredentialOptions`) are defined in `Azure.Identity`. This means each SDK library would need to take a **new direct dependency on Azure.Identity** to offer these extensions — adding a dependency to every SDK package that doesn't currently have one.

## Proposed Approach to Explore

Since all SDK libraries already depend on `Azure.Core`, and `Azure.Identity` already depends on `Azure.Core`, we should explore whether we can use [TypeForwardedTo](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.runtime.compilerservices.typeforwardedtoattribute) to make the relevant types available through `Azure.Core` without requiring a direct `Azure.Identity` dependency.

The high-level idea:
1. **Move** the types needed by `WithAzureCredential` from `Azure.Identity` into `Azure.Core`
2. **Add** `[assembly: TypeForwardedTo(...)]` attributes in `Azure.Identity` pointing to the new locations in `Azure.Core`
3. SDK libraries can then reference these types through their existing `Azure.Core` dependency
4. **Existing consumers of Azure.Identity are unaffected** — the runtime follows type forwarding transparently, so no breaking change occurs

This should not break any existing users of `Azure.Identity` since:
- `TypeForwardedTo` is a well-established .NET mechanism for moving types across assemblies
- `Azure.Core` is already in every SDK's dependency chain
- Existing compiled code referencing these types via `Azure.Identity` will be redirected at runtime

## Types That May Need to Move

Based on the current `WithAzureCredential` implementation in `Azure.Identity/src/ConfigurationExtensions.cs`, the following types are referenced:

| Type | Current Location | Notes |
|------|-----------------|-------|
| `ConfigurableCredential` | Azure.Identity (currently internal) | Extends `TokenCredential`; visibility may need adjustment |
| `ConfigurableCredentialCache` | Azure.Identity | Static caching layer for credential reuse |
| `DefaultAzureCredentialOptions` | Azure.Identity | Options class for credential configuration |
| `WithAzureCredential` extensions | Azure.Identity (`ConfigurationExtensions`) | The extension methods on `ClientSettings` and `IClientBuilder` |

## Open Questions and Risks

- **Internal types**: `ConfigurableCredential` is currently `internal` — `TypeForwardedTo` only works with public types, so the API surface may need to be adjusted or an alternative abstraction introduced
- **Transitive dependency concerns**: `ConfigurableCredential` has deep dependencies on MSAL libraries, `DefaultAzureCredential`, `ChainedTokenCredential`, etc. — moving it directly to `Azure.Core` could pull in unwanted transitive dependencies unless the design is refactored (e.g., using an interface/abstraction in Azure.Core that Azure.Identity implements)
- **Alternative patterns**: Instead of moving concrete types, it may be more appropriate to define an abstraction or factory pattern in Azure.Core that Azure.Identity fulfills at runtime
- **Existing precedent**: `TypeForwardedTo` is rarely used in this repo today (only 1 instance found in `Azure.Messaging.EventGrid`), so this would establish a new pattern

## Goal

Enable SDK libraries to provide `AddFooClient` extension methods with full `WithAzureCredential` support **without adding a new direct dependency on Azure.Identity**. This issue tracks the investigation of TypeForwarding as the primary approach, but alternative solutions may emerge during exploration.","author_association":"MEMBER","user":{"login":"m-nash","id":64171366,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjY0MTcxMzY2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/64171366?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/m-nash","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-nash","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-nash/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-nash/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-nash/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-nash/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-nash/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-nash/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-nash/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-nash/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/m-nash/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":1178447860,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Client","name":"Client","color":"ffeb77","description":"This Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent NullReferenceException after successful streamed response finalization","body":"## Bug description
When using `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` through Microsoft Agent Framework streaming APIs, a chat run can complete successfully, return the full assistant answer, and then crash during stream finalization/disposal with a `System.NullReferenceException`.

The exception originates inside the Azure SDK telemetry/finalization path:
`Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(...)`

## Package and environment
- Package: `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`
- Package version: `1.0.0-preview.260311.1`
- App shape: .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework with Azure Foundry persistent agents
- OS: Windows
- Auth=[REDACTED] Streaming path: `AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(...)`

## Expected behavior
After the streamed answer completes, enumeration/disposal should finish cleanly.

## Actual behavior
The full assistant answer is returned to the caller, but the process crashes afterward during stream cleanup/final telemetry processing.

## Reproduction notes
1. Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.
2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.
3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.
4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.

## Minimal observed output
```text
assistant(stream)> <full answer omitted>
[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...
[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...
[content:UsageContent] Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageContent
Unhandled exception. System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(Utf8JsonWriter writer, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel<Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall>.Write(...)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.ModelSerializationExtensions.WriteObjectValue[T](...)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.ToRequestContent()
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.GetToolCallAttributes(RunStepToolCall toolCall)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()
```

## Suspected root cause
The generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.

Relevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`

## Impact
- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.
- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.
- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.

## Workaround currently used
Application-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.

## Request

Design proper mapping for DataFactoryElement<T> in provisioning library","body":"## Summary

The current Azure.Provisioning.DataFactory generator maps `DataFactoryElement<T>` (from the `Azure.Core.Expressions.DataFactory` package) to `BicepValue<T>` by unwrapping the generic type argument. This mapping is incorrect and needs a proper design.

## Background

`DataFactoryElement<T>` is a special type in the Azure Data Factory SDK that represents values which can be one of:
- A **literal value** of type `T`
- A **Data Factory expression** (e.g., `@pipeline().parameters.myParam`)
- A **secret reference** (Azure Key Vault)

When we generate the provisioning library, the generator's `ResolveExternalGenericType` hook simply strips the `DataFactoryElement<T>` wrapper and uses `T` directly, which then becomes `BicepValue<T>` in the generated code. This loses the ability to express Data Factory expressions and secret references in the Bicep output.

## Current Behavior

`DataFactoryElement<string>` -> generator unwraps to `string` -> becomes `BicepValue<string>`

This means a property like `FilterActivity.Condition` (which is `DataFactoryElement<bool>`) becomes `BicepValue<bool>` and can only hold a literal boolean value - it cannot represent a Data Factory expression like `@equals(1, 1)`.

## Expected Behavior

We need a proper design that preserves the semantics of `DataFactoryElement<T>` in Bicep. Options to consider:

1. **Map to `BicepValue<BinaryData>`** - allows arbitrary JSON including expression objects, but loses type safety
2. **Create a custom `BicepDataFactoryExpression<T>`** - a provisioning-specific type that can serialize to either a literal `T` or a `{ value, type }` expression object
3. **Map to `BicepValue<string>`** always - since Data Factory expressions are string-based, always emit as string and let ADF runtime handle type coercion
4. **Other approaches** - open to discussion

## Affected Properties

Hundreds of properties across the Data Factory provisioning library use `DataFactoryElement<T>` in the management SDK, including:
- `FilterActivity.Condition` (`DataFactoryElement<bool>`)
- `FilterActivity.Items` (`DataFactoryElement<IList<DataFactoryElement<string>>>`)
- `CopyActivity.EnableStaging` (`DataFactoryElement<bool>`)
- Many linked service connection properties

## Related

- PR #57127 - Initial Azure.Provisioning.DataFactory library
- `Azure.Core.Expressions.DataFactory` package - defines `DataFactoryElement<T>`
- Generator code: {"total_count":6,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4103136491,"number":57254,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[Generator Bug] ModelFactory @@flattenProperty: ProcessTypeForBackCompatibility discards flattened property values when param order changes","body":"## Bug Summary

When a model has `@@flattenProperty` applied AND the previous SDK contract had the same flattened parameters in a **different order**, the generated ModelFactory method accepts flattened parameters but passes `default` for the Properties wrapper — **silently discarding all values**.

## Root Cause

A timing/ordering issue in the generator pipeline (`CSharpGen.cs`):

1. **`EnsureBuilt()`** — factory method has `properties` wrapper param
2. **`FlattenPropertyVisitor`** — correctly replaces `properties` with flattened sub-params AND builds the ternary `new Properties(...)` in the body ✅
3. **`ProcessTypeForBackCompatibility()`** — runs **after** all visitors, detects the old SDK had the same params in a different order, calls `TryBuildMethodArgumentsForOverload` which **replaces the method body** ❌

In step 3, `ConstructMethodBody` tries to rebuild the body by matching params by name, but `"properties"` doesn't match any flattened param name (e.g. `"provisioningState"`), so it passes `default` for the Properties wrapper.

## Affected ServiceBus Models

| Model | Factory creates wrapper? | Values discarded |
|-------|--------------------------|------------------|
| `ServiceBusNamespaceData` | ✅ Correct | None |
| `ServiceBusPrivateEndpointConnectionData` | ❌ `default` | `privateEndpointId`, `connectionState`, `provisioningState` |
| `ServiceBusNamespacePatch` | ❌ `default` | `provisioningState`, `status`, `createdOn`, `updatedOn`, `serviceBusEndpoint`, `metricId`, `encryption`, `privateEndpointConnections`, `disableLocalAuth`, `alternateName` |

## Trigger Condition

The bug is triggered when:
- `@@flattenProperty` is applied to a model's `properties` field
- The old SDK (baseline) had the **exact same** flattened params in a **different order**
- No new params were added (equal count)

## Suggested Fix

In `ModelFactoryProvider.cs` method `TryBuildMethodArgumentsForOverload` (approx line 258):

`csharp
// Current: rejects equal-count reordered params
if (currentMethodParameterCount <= previousParameterCount || ...)
    return false;

// Fix: change <= to < to allow equal-count reordering
if (currentMethodParameterCount < previousParameterCount || ...)
    return false;
`

Plus use name-based parameter comparison instead of reference equality in the HashSet.

## Workaround

For `ServiceBusNamespacePatch`: reorder the `properties` field placement in the TypeSpec replacement model (move it after the `...OmitProperties<>` spread).

For `ServiceBusPrivateEndpointConnectionData`: the replacement model approach doesn't work because `@@alternateType` on an ARM resource strips its resource identity. Must use a Customized partial class to override the factory method.

## Related

- ServiceBus TypeSpec migration PR: #57113
- Spec PR: Generator] BuildGetChildResourceMethods does not pass path parameters for nested child resources","body":"## Bug Description

`ResourceClientProvider.BuildGetChildResourceMethods()` generates collection getter and `Get`/`GetAsync` methods for child resources on a parent resource class. When child resources have extra path parameters between the parent and child resource (e.g., `nestedResourceTypeFirst`), these parameters are:

1. **Not passed to the collection constructor** in the getter method body (line 480)
2. **Not included in the Get/GetAsync method signatures** (line 504-510)
3. **Not forwarded** when calling the collection getter from Get/GetAsync methods (line 515)

## Impact

This affects any ARM resource with nested children that have intermediate path segments. For example, ProviderHub has:
- `ResourceTypeRegistration` (parent)
  - `NestedResourceTypeFirstSku` (child, needs `nestedResourceTypeFirst` path param)
  - `NestedResourceTypeSecondSku` (child, needs `nestedResourceTypeFirst` + `nestedResourceTypeSecond`)
  - `NestedResourceTypeThirdSku` (child, needs 3 path params)

The generated accessor methods either compile with errors or are suppressed via `CodeGenSuppress`.

## Root Cause

**File**: `ResourceClientProvider.cs` in `Azure.Generator.Management`

**Collection getter** (line 480):
\\\csharp
// Only passes client and Id, ignores path parameters from signature
New.Instance(childResource.ResourceCollection.Type, client, thisResource.Id())
\\\

**Get method signature** (line 504-510): Only includes `collectionSignature.Parameters` (e.g., sku, cancellationToken) but not the path parameters from the collection getter's signature.

**Get method body** (line 515): Calls `thisResource.Invoke(signature.Name)` without passing path parameter arguments.

## Fix

See PR linked to this issue.","author_association":"MEMBER","user":{"login":"haiyuazhang","id":1924967,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE5MjQ5Njc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1924967?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/haiyuazhang","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/subscriptions"},"comments":0,"created_at":"2026-03-19T17:17:55Z","updated_at":"2026-03-19T17:17:55Z","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252/events","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252/labels{/name}","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252/reactions"},"node_id":"I_kwDOACyxMM70kE5B"},{"id":4102778129,"number":57250,"state":"closed","state_reason":"not_planned","locked":true,"title":"Easy","body":"### Type of issue

ApiSchemaTests.CRUD playback test failure due to recording mismatch","body":"## Description

The `ApiSchemaTests.CRUD` test in `Azure.ResourceManager.ApiManagement` is failing in playback mode with `TestRecordingMismatchException`. This indicates that the recorded session is out of date and needs to be re-recorded.

## Error

```
Azure.Core.TestFramework.TestRecordingMismatchException : If this is a new recording, make sure you have pushed the recordings to the assets repository.
   at Azure.ResourceManager.ApiManagement.Tests.ApiSchemaTests.CRUD() in sdk/apimanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.ApiManagement/tests/Scenario/ApiSchemaTests.cs:line 242
```

## Impact

This test failure blocks CI for unrelated PRs that include the `apimanagement` service directory.

## Action Required

Re-record the `ApiSchemaTests.CRUD` test session and push updated recordings to the assets repository.

## Affected File

- Tag methods should not be generated when PATCH operation has no request body","body":"## Description

The current MPG (Management Plane Generator) does not check whether a PATCH operation accepts a request body before deciding to generate tag methods (`AddTag`/`SetTags`/`RemoveTag`). If a resource has a `tags` property (e.g. inherits from `TrackedResource`) and has a PATCH operation that takes **no request body**, the generator may still produce tag methods — but those methods would be non-functional since there is no way to write updated tags back to the service via the bodyless PATCH.

## Current Behavior

In `ResourceClientProvider.BuildMethods()` ([ResourceClientProvider.cs#L409](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/ResourceClientProvider.cs#L409)), the tag generation gate is:

```csharp
if (HasTags() && _readMethod is not null && updateMethodProvider is not null)
```

`updateMethodProvider` is set to non-null whenever a `ResourceOperationKind.Create` (PUT) or `ResourceOperationKind.Update` (PATCH) method exists (line 382), **regardless of whether the PATCH operation accepts a body parameter**.

The fallback path in `BaseTagMethodProvider.BuildElseStatements()` ([BaseTagMethodProvider.cs#L228](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/TagMethodProviders/BaseTagMethodProvider.cs#L228)) then does:

```csharp
var updateParam = _updateMethodProvider.Signature.Parameters
    .Where(p => !p.Type.Equals(typeof(WaitUntil))).First();
```

This assumes a body parameter exists. For a bodyless PATCH, this would either crash or produce nonsensical code.

## Expected Behavior

The generator should **skip tag method generation** when:
- The resource has no PUT (`CreateOrUpdate`) operation, **and**
- The PATCH (`Update`) operation has **no request body** (i.e. no parameter to carry tag changes)

## Evidence from Existing SDKs

Analysis of the existing generated SDKs shows this pattern clearly. Among the 34 `TrackedResourceData` resources without tag methods in the current repo:

| Category | Count |
|---|---|
| No PATCH at all | 16 |
| PATCH exists but has no body | 16 |
| Has full CRUD (anomaly, AppService only) | 2 |

**None** of the 34 have a PATCH with a body. Resources with bodyless PATCHes include:
- `ProviderMonitorSettingResource` (ProviderHub) — `UpdateAsync(CancellationToken)`
- `AppServiceCertificateOrderResource` (CertificateRegistration)
- `CosmosDBFleetResource` (CosmosDB)
- `LabPlanResource` / `LabResource` (LabServices)
- And 11 others

In contrast, **all 568** `TrackedResourceData` resources that correctly have tag methods also have either a PUT or a PATCH **with a body parameter**.

## Suggested Fix

In `ResourceClientProvider.BuildMethods()`, add a check that the update method has a meaningful body parameter before generating tag methods. For example, in `PopulateUpdateClient()` or at the tag generation gate, verify that the PATCH method's input model contains parameters beyond `WaitUntil` and `CancellationToken`.","author_association":"MEMBER","user":{"login":"haiyuazhang","id":1924967,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE5MjQ5Njc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1924967?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/haiyuazhang","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":1178448111,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Mgmt","name":"Mgmt","color":"ffeb77","description":"This UpdateAnalyzer/UpdateAnalyzerAsync docs should clarify which properties are updatable","body":"## Description

The `UpdateAnalyzer` and `UpdateAnalyzerAsync` methods (and their protocol overloads) currently have XML doc summaries that say only "Update analyzer properties" without specifying **which** properties can actually be updated.

The underlying REST API uses `PATCH` with `application/merge-patch+json`, and **only `description` and `tags`** are accepted by the service. All other properties (`models`, `fieldSchema`, `config`, `baseAnalyzerId`, `dynamicFieldSchema`, `processingLocation`, `knowledgeSources`) are create-only — they are silently ignored by the server even though it returns HTTP 200.

This has caused customer confusion — see #57215.

## Requested Improvements

1. **API doc (`<summary>` / `<remarks>`)**: Add a `<remarks>` section to `UpdateAnalyzer`/`UpdateAnalyzerAsync` listing the updatable properties and noting that other properties require `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true`.

2. **Sample doc (Sample08_UpdateAnalyzer.md)**: Add a note in the "About updating analyzers" section explaining that only `description` and `tags` are updatable via this method, and reference `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true` for changing `models`, `fieldSchema`, etc.

## Updatable Properties

| Property | Updatable via `UpdateAnalyzer` (PATCH)? |
|---|---|
| `description` | **Yes** |
| `tags` | **Yes** |
| `models` | No — silently ignored |
| `fieldSchema` | No — silently ignored |
| `config` | No — silently ignored |
| `baseAnalyzerId` | No — silently ignored |
| `dynamicFieldSchema` | No — silently ignored |
| `processingLocation` | No — silently ignored |
| `knowledgeSources` | No — silently ignored |

## Workaround

To change create-only properties on an existing analyzer, use `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true`. This calls `PUT` (full resource replacement) instead of `PATCH`.

## Related

- #57215 — customer report of `UpdateAnalyzerAsync` not updating `models`

{"total_count":10,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4103136491,"number":57254,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[Generator Bug] ModelFactory @@flattenProperty: ProcessTypeForBackCompatibility discards flattened property values when param order changes","body":"## Bug Summary

When a model has `@@flattenProperty` applied AND the previous SDK contract had the same flattened parameters in a **different order**, the generated ModelFactory method accepts flattened parameters but passes `default` for the Properties wrapper — **silently discarding all values**.

## Root Cause

A timing/ordering issue in the generator pipeline (`CSharpGen.cs`):

1. **`EnsureBuilt()`** — factory method has `properties` wrapper param
2. **`FlattenPropertyVisitor`** — correctly replaces `properties` with flattened sub-params AND builds the ternary `new Properties(...)` in the body ✅
3. **`ProcessTypeForBackCompatibility()`** — runs **after** all visitors, detects the old SDK had the same params in a different order, calls `TryBuildMethodArgumentsForOverload` which **replaces the method body** ❌

In step 3, `ConstructMethodBody` tries to rebuild the body by matching params by name, but `"properties"` doesn't match any flattened param name (e.g. `"provisioningState"`), so it passes `default` for the Properties wrapper.

## Affected ServiceBus Models

| Model | Factory creates wrapper? | Values discarded |
|-------|--------------------------|------------------|
| `ServiceBusNamespaceData` | ✅ Correct | None |
| `ServiceBusPrivateEndpointConnectionData` | ❌ `default` | `privateEndpointId`, `connectionState`, `provisioningState` |
| `ServiceBusNamespacePatch` | ❌ `default` | `provisioningState`, `status`, `createdOn`, `updatedOn`, `serviceBusEndpoint`, `metricId`, `encryption`, `privateEndpointConnections`, `disableLocalAuth`, `alternateName` |

## Trigger Condition

The bug is triggered when:
- `@@flattenProperty` is applied to a model's `properties` field
- The old SDK (baseline) had the **exact same** flattened params in a **different order**
- No new params were added (equal count)

## Suggested Fix

In `ModelFactoryProvider.cs` method `TryBuildMethodArgumentsForOverload` (approx line 258):

`csharp
// Current: rejects equal-count reordered params
if (currentMethodParameterCount <= previousParameterCount || ...)
    return false;

// Fix: change <= to < to allow equal-count reordering
if (currentMethodParameterCount < previousParameterCount || ...)
    return false;
`

Plus use name-based parameter comparison instead of reference equality in the HashSet.

## Workaround

For `ServiceBusNamespacePatch`: reorder the `properties` field placement in the TypeSpec replacement model (move it after the `...OmitProperties<>` spread).

For `ServiceBusPrivateEndpointConnectionData`: the replacement model approach doesn't work because `@@alternateType` on an ARM resource strips its resource identity. Must use a Customized partial class to override the factory method.

## Related

- ServiceBus TypeSpec migration PR: #57113
- Spec PR: Generator] BuildGetChildResourceMethods does not pass path parameters for nested child resources","body":"## Bug Description

`ResourceClientProvider.BuildGetChildResourceMethods()` generates collection getter and `Get`/`GetAsync` methods for child resources on a parent resource class. When child resources have extra path parameters between the parent and child resource (e.g., `nestedResourceTypeFirst`), these parameters are:

1. **Not passed to the collection constructor** in the getter method body (line 480)
2. **Not included in the Get/GetAsync method signatures** (line 504-510)
3. **Not forwarded** when calling the collection getter from Get/GetAsync methods (line 515)

## Impact

This affects any ARM resource with nested children that have intermediate path segments. For example, ProviderHub has:
- `ResourceTypeRegistration` (parent)
  - `NestedResourceTypeFirstSku` (child, needs `nestedResourceTypeFirst` path param)
  - `NestedResourceTypeSecondSku` (child, needs `nestedResourceTypeFirst` + `nestedResourceTypeSecond`)
  - `NestedResourceTypeThirdSku` (child, needs 3 path params)

The generated accessor methods either compile with errors or are suppressed via `CodeGenSuppress`.

## Root Cause

**File**: `ResourceClientProvider.cs` in `Azure.Generator.Management`

**Collection getter** (line 480):
\\\csharp
// Only passes client and Id, ignores path parameters from signature
New.Instance(childResource.ResourceCollection.Type, client, thisResource.Id())
\\\

**Get method signature** (line 504-510): Only includes `collectionSignature.Parameters` (e.g., sku, cancellationToken) but not the path parameters from the collection getter's signature.

**Get method body** (line 515): Calls `thisResource.Invoke(signature.Name)` without passing path parameter arguments.

## Fix

ApiSchemaTests.CRUD playback test failure due to recording mismatch","body":"## Description

The `ApiSchemaTests.CRUD` test in `Azure.ResourceManager.ApiManagement` is failing in playback mode with `TestRecordingMismatchException`. This indicates that the recorded session is out of date and needs to be re-recorded.

## Error

```
Azure.Core.TestFramework.TestRecordingMismatchException : If this is a new recording, make sure you have pushed the recordings to the assets repository.
   at Azure.ResourceManager.ApiManagement.Tests.ApiSchemaTests.CRUD() in sdk/apimanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.ApiManagement/tests/Scenario/ApiSchemaTests.cs:line 242
```

## Impact

This test failure blocks CI for unrelated PRs that include the `apimanagement` service directory.

## Action Required

Re-record the `ApiSchemaTests.CRUD` test session and push updated recordings to the assets repository.

Tag methods should not be generated when PATCH operation has no request body","body":"## Description

The current MPG (Management Plane Generator) does not check whether a PATCH operation accepts a request body before deciding to generate tag methods (`AddTag`/`SetTags`/`RemoveTag`). If a resource has a `tags` property (e.g. inherits from `TrackedResource`) and has a PATCH operation that takes **no request body**, the generator may still produce tag methods — but those methods would be non-functional since there is no way to write updated tags back to the service via the bodyless PATCH.

## Current Behavior

In `ResourceClientProvider.BuildMethods()` ([ResourceClientProvider.cs#L409](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/ResourceClientProvider.cs#L409)), the tag generation gate is:

```csharp
if (HasTags() && _readMethod is not null && updateMethodProvider is not null)
```

`updateMethodProvider` is set to non-null whenever a `ResourceOperationKind.Create` (PUT) or `ResourceOperationKind.Update` (PATCH) method exists (line 382), **regardless of whether the PATCH operation accepts a body parameter**.

The fallback path in `BaseTagMethodProvider.BuildElseStatements()` ([BaseTagMethodProvider.cs#L228](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/TagMethodProviders/BaseTagMethodProvider.cs#L228)) then does:

```csharp
var updateParam = _updateMethodProvider.Signature.Parameters
    .Where(p => !p.Type.Equals(typeof(WaitUntil))).First();
```

This assumes a body parameter exists. For a bodyless PATCH, this would either crash or produce nonsensical code.

## Expected Behavior

The generator should **skip tag method generation** when:
- The resource has no PUT (`CreateOrUpdate`) operation, **and**
- The PATCH (`Update`) operation has **no request body** (i.e. no parameter to carry tag changes)

## Evidence from Existing SDKs

Analysis of the existing generated SDKs shows this pattern clearly. Among the 34 `TrackedResourceData` resources without tag methods in the current repo:

| Category | Count |
|---|---|
| No PATCH at all | 16 |
| PATCH exists but has no body | 16 |
| Has full CRUD (anomaly, AppService only) | 2 |

**None** of the 34 have a PATCH with a body. Resources with bodyless PATCHes include:
- `ProviderMonitorSettingResource` (ProviderHub) — `UpdateAsync(CancellationToken)`
- `AppServiceCertificateOrderResource` (CertificateRegistration)
- `CosmosDBFleetResource` (CosmosDB)
- `LabPlanResource` / `LabResource` (LabServices)
- And 11 others

In contrast, **all 568** `TrackedResourceData` resources that correctly have tag methods also have either a PUT or a PATCH **with a body parameter**.

## Suggested Fix

In `ResourceClientProvider.BuildMethods()`, add a check that the update method has a meaningful body parameter before generating tag methods. For example, in `PopulateUpdateClient()` or at the tag generation gate, verify that the PATCH method's input model contains parameters beyond `WaitUntil` and UpdateAnalyzer/UpdateAnalyzerAsync docs should clarify which properties are updatable","body":"## Description

The `UpdateAnalyzer` and `UpdateAnalyzerAsync` methods (and their protocol overloads) currently have XML doc summaries that say only "Update analyzer properties" without specifying **which** properties can actually be updated.

The underlying REST API uses `PATCH` with `application/merge-patch+json`, and **only `description` and `tags`** are accepted by the service. All other properties (`models`, `fieldSchema`, `config`, `baseAnalyzerId`, `dynamicFieldSchema`, `processingLocation`, `knowledgeSources`) are create-only — they are silently ignored by the server even though it returns HTTP 200.

This has caused customer confusion — see #57215.

## Requested Improvements

1. **API doc (`<summary>` / `<remarks>`)**: Add a `<remarks>` section to `UpdateAnalyzer`/`UpdateAnalyzerAsync` listing the updatable properties and noting that other properties require `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true`.

2. **Sample doc (Sample08_UpdateAnalyzer.md)**: Add a note in the "About updating analyzers" section explaining that only `description` and `tags` are updatable via this method, and reference `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true` for changing `models`, `fieldSchema`, etc.

## Updatable Properties

| Property | Updatable via `UpdateAnalyzer` (PATCH)? |
|---|---|
| `description` | **Yes** |
| `tags` | **Yes** |
| `models` | No — silently ignored |
| `fieldSchema` | No — silently ignored |
| `config` | No — silently ignored |
| `baseAnalyzerId` | No — silently ignored |
| `dynamicFieldSchema` | No — silently ignored |
| `processingLocation` | No — silently ignored |
| `knowledgeSources` | No — silently ignored |

## Workaround

To change create-only properties on an existing analyzer, use `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true`. This calls `PUT` (full resource replacement) instead of `PATCH`.

## Related

- #57215 — customer report of `UpdateAnalyzerAsync` not updating `models`

When a multi-path ARM resource (same model used at multiple URL paths) has its operations routed to a parent client via `@@clientLocation`, the **List operation** is incorrectly assigned to the **parent resource** instead of the child resource. CRUD operations (Create, Read, Delete) and Action operations are correctly assigned.

## Root Cause

In `resource-detection.ts`, the emitter uses a two-pass approach:
- **Pass 1**: CRUD operations (Read, Create, Update, Delete) establish resource paths
- **Pass 2**: Non-CRUD operations (List, Action) are matched to existing resources via prefix matching

The prefix matching logic at approximately line 195-199:
```typescript
const bestPrefixMatch = findLongestPrefixMatch(
  operationPath,
  existingPathsForModel,
  (path) => path.substring(0, path.lastIndexOf("/"))
);
```

strips the last segment from existing resource paths for comparison. For List operations, this causes the child resource's path to collapse into the parent resource's path, so the List gets assigned to the parent.

## Reproduction

**Spec**: ServiceBus (`specification/servicebus/resource-manager/Microsoft.ServiceBus/ServiceBus`)

The `SBAuthorizationRule` model is used at 4 different paths (multi-path resource):
- `/namespaces/{name}/authorizationRules/{ruleName}` (Namespace scope)
- `/namespaces/{name}/queues/{queue}/authorizationRules/{ruleName}` (Queue scope)
- `/namespaces/{name}/topics/{topic}/authorizationRules/{ruleName}` (Topic scope)
- `/namespaces/{name}/disasterRecoveryConfigs/{alias}/authorizationRules/{ruleName}` (DR scope)

The Namespace-scope operations are routed via `@@clientLocation` to the `"Namespaces"` client in `back-compatible.tsp`:
```
@@clientLocation(SBAuthorizationRules.getAuthorizationRule, "Namespaces");
@@clientLocation(SBAuthorizationRules.createOrUpdateAuthorizationRule, "Namespaces");
@@clientLocation(SBAuthorizationRules.deleteAuthorizationRule, "Namespaces");
@@clientLocation(SBAuthorizationRules.listAuthorizationRules, "Namespaces");
```

With `OverrideResourceName = "ServiceBusNamespaceAuthorizationRule"` set on all CRUD operations via `LegacyOperations` template.

**Result in `armProviderSchema`:**

✅ CRUD + Action ops correctly go to `ServiceBusNamespaceAuthorizationRule`:
```
resourceName: ServiceBusNamespaceAuthorizationRule
  methods: [Create, Read, Delete, Action:listKeys, Action:regenerateKeys]
```

❌ List op incorrectly goes to `ServiceBusNamespace`:
```
resourceName: ServiceBusNamespace
  methods: [..., List:listAuthorizationRules, ...]
```

**Why Actions work but List doesn't:**
- Action paths include the instance ID: `.../authorizationRules/{ruleName}/listKeys` → prefix matches auth rule resource ✅
- List path does NOT include instance ID: `.../authorizationRules` → after `lastIndexOf("/")` stripping, matches Namespace resource ❌

## Impact

The generated `ServiceBusNamespaceAuthorizationRuleCollection` is missing:
- `IEnumerable<ServiceBusNamespaceAuthorizationRuleResource>` interface
- `IAsyncEnumerable<ServiceBusNamespaceAuthorizationRuleResource>` interface  
- `GetAll()` / `GetAllAsync()` methods

Other scopes (Queue, Topic, DR) that have their own dedicated interfaces (not routed via `@@clientLocation`) generate correctly with all of the above.

## Expected Behavior

The List operation `listAuthorizationRules` should be assigned to `ServiceBusNamespaceAuthorizationRule` resource (matching the CRUD operations), so the generated Collection class includes `IEnumerable`, `GetAll`, and `GetAllAsync`.

## Suggested Fix

The prefix matching logic for List operations should consider the **resource type** extracted from the operation path, not just the path prefix. A List operation at `.../authorizationRules` should match the resource with type `Microsoft.ServiceBus/namespaces/authorizationRules`, not the parent `Microsoft.ServiceBus/namespaces`.

## Environment

- Emitter: `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt@1.0.0-alpha.20260314.1`
- File: Update of analyzer is not working properly","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding 1.0.2

### Describe the bug

Hi,
When trying to update chat completion model in C# with Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding 1.0.2 NuGet package the update is not working properly. I am attaching a sample code:
```
protected async Task<string> ChangeDefaultChatModel(ContentUnderstandingClient client, string analyzerId, string defaultModel)
{
    var analyzer = (await client.GetAnalyzerAsync(analyzerId)).Value;

    analyzer.Models["completion"] = defaultModel; 

    await client.UpdateAnalyzerAsync(analyzerId, analyzer);

    return defaultModel;
}
```
The completion model is not updated. When I execute the code 2nd time it is not changed. I was trying to change the completion model from "gpt-4.1" to "gpt-4.1-mini" (both are supported models). The completion model stays set to "gpt-4.1" all the time even after the update (probably unsuccessful).

I had to make a PowerShell script to update the analyzer instead. Update with my custom PowerShell was working fine. Update with the NuGet is failing.


### Expected behavior

The chat completion model should be updated to the desired model - e.g. "gpt-4.1-mini".

### Actual behavior

The chat completion model is not changed properly and it is still the old one "gpt-4.1".

### Reproduction Steps

Execute the method from the problem description:
ChangeDefaultChatModel(client, "analyzer-id", "gpt-4.1-mini")
Analyzer's chat completion model will not be updated.

Emitter] List operation prefix matching incorrectly assigns child resource list to parent resource","body":"## Bug Description

When a multi-path ARM resource (same model used at multiple URL paths) has its operations routed to a parent client via `@@clientLocation`, the **List operation** is incorrectly assigned to the **parent resource** instead of the child resource. CRUD operations (Create, Read, Delete) and Action operations are correctly assigned.

## Root Cause

In `resource-detection.ts`, the emitter uses a two-pass approach:
- **Pass 1**: CRUD operations (Read, Create, Update, Delete) establish resource paths
- **Pass 2**: Non-CRUD operations (List, Action) are matched to existing resources via prefix matching

The prefix matching logic at approximately line 195-199:
```typescript
const bestPrefixMatch = findLongestPrefixMatch(
  operationPath,
  existingPathsForModel,
  (path) => path.substring(0, path.lastIndexOf("/"))
);
```

strips the last segment from existing resource paths for comparison. For List operations, this causes the child resource's path to collapse into the parent resource's path, so the List gets assigned to the parent.

## Reproduction

**Spec**: ServiceBus (`specification/servicebus/resource-manager/Microsoft.ServiceBus/ServiceBus`)

The `SBAuthorizationRule` model is used at 4 different paths (multi-path resource):
- `/namespaces/{name}/authorizationRules/{ruleName}` (Namespace scope)
- `/namespaces/{name}/queues/{queue}/authorizationRules/{ruleName}` (Queue scope)
- `/namespaces/{name}/topics/{topic}/authorizationRules/{ruleName}` (Topic scope)
- `/namespaces/{name}/disasterRecoveryConfigs/{alias}/authorizationRules/{ruleName}` (DR scope)

The Namespace-scope operations are routed via `@@clientLocation` to the `"Namespaces"` client in `back-compatible.tsp`:
```
@@clientLocation(SBAuthorizationRules.getAuthorizationRule, "Namespaces");
@@clientLocation(SBAuthorizationRules.createOrUpdateAuthorizationRule, "Namespaces");
@@clientLocation(SBAuthorizationRules.deleteAuthorizationRule, "Namespaces");
@@clientLocation(SBAuthorizationRules.listAuthorizationRules, "Namespaces");
```

With `OverrideResourceName = "ServiceBusNamespaceAuthorizationRule"` set on all CRUD operations via `LegacyOperations` template.

**Result in `armProviderSchema`:**

✅ CRUD + Action ops correctly go to `ServiceBusNamespaceAuthorizationRule`:
```
resourceName: ServiceBusNamespaceAuthorizationRule
  methods: [Create, Read, Delete, Action:listKeys, Action:regenerateKeys]
```

❌ List op incorrectly goes to `ServiceBusNamespace`:
```
resourceName: ServiceBusNamespace
  methods: [..., List:listAuthorizationRules, ...]
```

**Why Actions work but List doesn't:**
- Action paths include the instance ID: `.../authorizationRules/{ruleName}/listKeys` → prefix matches auth rule resource ✅
- List path does NOT include instance ID: `.../authorizationRules` → after `lastIndexOf("/")` stripping, matches Namespace resource ❌

## Impact

The generated `ServiceBusNamespaceAuthorizationRuleCollection` is missing:
- `IEnumerable<ServiceBusNamespaceAuthorizationRuleResource>` interface
- `IAsyncEnumerable<ServiceBusNamespaceAuthorizationRuleResource>` interface  
- `GetAll()` / `GetAllAsync()` methods

Other scopes (Queue, Topic, DR) that have their own dedicated interfaces (not routed via `@@clientLocation`) generate correctly with all of the above.

## Expected Behavior

The List operation `listAuthorizationRules` should be assigned to `ServiceBusNamespaceAuthorizationRule` resource (matching the CRUD operations), so the generated Collection class includes `IEnumerable`, `GetAll`, and `GetAllAsync`.

## Suggested Fix

The prefix matching logic for List operations should consider the **resource type** extracted from the operation path, not just the path prefix. A List operation at `.../authorizationRules` should match the resource with type `Microsoft.ServiceBus/namespaces/authorizationRules`, not the parent `Microsoft.ServiceBus/namespaces`.

## Environment

- Emitter: `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt@1.0.0-alpha.20260314.1`
- File: ### Describe the bug

Hi,
When trying to update chat completion model in C# with Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding 1.0.2 NuGet package the update is not working properly. I am attaching a sample code:
```
protected async Task<string> ChangeDefaultChatModel(ContentUnderstandingClient client, string analyzerId, string defaultModel)
{
    var analyzer = (await client.GetAnalyzerAsync(analyzerId)).Value;

    analyzer.Models["completion"] = defaultModel; 

    await client.UpdateAnalyzerAsync(analyzerId, analyzer);

    return defaultModel;
}
```
The completion model is not updated. When I execute the code 2nd time it is not changed. I was trying to change the completion model from "gpt-4.1" to "gpt-4.1-mini" (both are supported models). The completion model stays set to "gpt-4.1" all the time even after the update (probably unsuccessful).

I had to make a PowerShell script to update the analyzer instead. Update with my custom PowerShell was working fine. Update with the NuGet is failing.


### Expected behavior

The chat completion model should be updated to the desired model - e.g. "gpt-4.1-mini".

### Actual behavior

The chat completion model is not changed properly and it is still the old one "gpt-4.1".

### Reproduction Steps

Execute the method from the problem description:
ChangeDefaultChatModel(client, "analyzer-id", "gpt-4.1-mini")
Analyzer's chat completion model will not be updated.
